reforms.description: Poland plans to strengthen science and higher education by restoring university autonomy and raising funding for R&D, including an increase in direct funding for ICT R&D. Additionally, generous tax incentives for R&D investment have been introduced, allowing firms to deduct up to 200% of R&D costs, which aims to enhance the innovation capacity of firms and boost the share of companies innovating in products or processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in R&D funding and tax incentives is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into effect as of 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change in R&D funding and incentives.
reforms.source_quote: Increase direct funding for ICT R&D. R&D increased to 1.5% of GDP in 2022 but the share of companies innovating either in terms of product or processes remains well below the EU average.

reforms.description: Poland introduced the possibility for start-ups and companies with no initial profit to carry over R&D tax incentives, allowing them to benefit from these incentives even if they do not generate immediate profits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is designed to enhance the financial accessibility of R&D for startups, thereby fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been implemented as of 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is a narrower adjustment to existing tax incentives.
reforms.source_quote: Introduce the possibility to carry-over R&D tax incentives so that start-ups and companies with no initial profit can also benefit.

reforms.description: Publicly financed support for startups has continued, providing essential financial backing for new digital firms, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the startup ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This support is vital for the growth of innovative firms, enhancing their ability to contribute to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The support has been ongoing and is confirmed as implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The continuation of support is significant but does not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Provide continued financial support to new digital firms including finance and development at a later stage.

reforms.description: Poland plans to adopt a national strategy for public integrity, which aims to strengthen the governance of state-owned enterprises and improve the business environment, thereby enhancing productivity and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving governance and the business environment is expected to foster innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant structural change in governance and business practices.
reforms.source_quote: Adopting a national strategy for public integrity and strengthening the implementation of the framework would improve the business environment.

reforms.description: The Polish Development Fund has co-invested alongside private equity funds, significantly increasing venture capital funding for new and innovative firms, which aims to enhance the availability of finance for startups and foster innovation in the business sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to significantly improve access to finance for startups, thereby promoting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been confirmed as implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While impactful, this initiative is a continuation of existing efforts rather than a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Funding has increased by more than ten-fold since 2018 to around PLN 2 billion in 2023.

reforms.description: Poland's Integrated Skills Strategy 2030 aims to enhance adult education and training, particularly targeting low-skilled workers and small businesses, to improve overall workforce skills and productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving workforce skills is essential for enhancing productivity and innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been confirmed as implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant structural change in workforce development.
reforms.source_quote: The latest policy strategy ('Integrated Skills Strategy 2030'), adopted in 2020, builds on an earlier analysis in the OECD Skills Strategy.
